item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images used in this article are cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0), as the first one was author-taken and the second one comes from Wikimedia Commons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a sucker for GAW's Little Misters, and despite how cliche'd it might be for me to say so, I genuinely didn't expect the Spanish Inquisition. I had a few gripes with the skip, but people already pointed them out in a better way than I could and this article is a few years old, anyways. +1 nonetheless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh golly! GAW has a series of misters?! This GoI just keeps getting better and better, and I seriously wasn't expecting that. Upvoted with a smile.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am disappointed in the lack of fatal heart attacks. I still upvoted though.

item.title: INTERVIEW RE-WRITE
item.content:encoded:
I'm thinking about replacing the interview in the second document with something new. It's in the collapsible below. What does everyone think?

+ Show whatever
- Hide whatever

Begin Log

SCP-2476: Ello! We’re gonna start another interview?

Dr. Belle: Yes, if that’s alright with you.

SCP-2476: Good! Good! What did ya wanna talk about?

Dr. Belle: We were hoping to discuss the creation of your anomalous properties.

SCP-2476: Really? Well…. God. I honestly have no clue what to tell you (sniggers). I wouldn’t really know what was going on in those guys heads at the time when they made me. They probablies just poured a whole bag of cocaine on a table and just started shoving their heads into it like (SCP-XXXX raises its head into the air and wines its eyes) “Aaaaaagghhhhh’!

Dr. Belle: You seem quite energetic today.

SCP-2476: Haha! (higher and squeakier) I know! …Can I have my outfit back?

Dr. Belle: Sorry, but I told you before, that's not possible.

SCP-2476: Oh, poooh!

Dr. Belle: But in all seriousness, what do you believe was the point of your creation? So far all other Misters have been made with at least some other meaningful purpose or show it as a desire. Not to say you yourself are pointless. We are just having a difficult time understanding why you were made like this.

SCP-2476: I don’t know really. I think it was less off “We’re gonna make something meaningful to make people think. Yeah we’re edgey and deep yeah!” and more like “Guys let’s just do this, it’ll be fucking incredible.”. Or maybe like a reverse thing? Like it’s a clever idea presented in a really stupid way?

Dr. Belle: It would seem that way. Speaking of, how do you feel during your well, episodes?

SCP-2476: It’s really weird! It’s not like I don’t remember doing it. It’s like when your really hyper you know? When you know you’re doing crazy things and you’re just in that zone of crazy? it’s like that but by one-hundred. I just go mental and like going around like ‘Ha ha!’

Dr. Belle: And how do you feel after the experience?

SCP-2476: Normally I just laugh at it. But honestly, sometimes I look back on it and am like “Oh God… What the hell we’re you doing!? You fool!”. Those surveillance tapes you showed me were pretty funny though! The peoples reactions alone were enough to cheer me up, so thanks for that.

Dr. Belle: Glad that helped you. Another question, how were you able to know about gamers against weed? When we asked about your earliest memories, you said you'd woken up in ██████.

SCP-2476: I remember some things from what I guess you'd call 'before'. They're not really clear memories, more like information I woke up with. I think whatever process they used to create me involved making me partly, then editing aspects of me while I was partly conscious… to see how I behaved or… something… Um…

Dr. Belle: Do you have a problem remembering? We can continue this at a later date.

SCP-2476: Nah nah it's good. It’s just a bit difficult to talk about certain parts.

SCP-2476: Let’s see… Agh! Fuck, this is complicated! The information I know was from what they mentioned when they were making me, kind of like how when you're a baby you take in things you hear people say. Except you know, i’m a fully grown guy with an almost fully developed brain (laughs) I do remember watching the Holy Grail like a thousand times though…

Dr. Belle: Well thank you, You've managed to give us a lot of helpful information.

SCP-2476: Hehe, you saying you didn't expect it?

Dr. Belle: Ok, you need to settle down.

<End Log>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, but i noticed a few mistakes and my pedantic nature prevents me from not mentioning them.

+ Let's do this
- Hide 

a cognihazardous affect

This should be effect, unless you mean a cognitohazardous facial expression, which actually is a great idea for a scip, DO NOT STEAL!!

26. Mr. Mad About Video Games

It's actually Ms. Mad About Video Games.

SIR-2476 is kept in a humanoid containment tower

While not incorrect, this made me think of a human shaped tower, which is another great idea, DO NOT STEAL!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No one expected the Spanish Inquisition!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I laughed at this way harder than I should have. And that twist was genuinely interesting and a bit mysterious, so I have to say

+ DON'T SAY IT
- (Ugh).

I didn't expect it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the idea, but the overall twist "Nobody expects the Spanish Inquisition" was lost on me as I had never even heard of MPATHG before Mr. Meme. The interview was ok.

EDIT: After rereading again, I gotta say I really like it now. My previous impression was that the cognitohazard influenced how a subject sees 2476 instead of information about 2476, which is entirely my fault.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea i had was the original document was written by someone under its affects. The document itself isn't causing an affect, just written under the influence of one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm largely having the same problems as Shaggy. The description of the violent behaviors weren't particularly clear or imaginable. It seems like trying to write up the skit (which I've seen) in clinical tone with perfect detail just probably isn't going to make sense. I'd try to simplify. Don't make everything hard and fast rules (it only knows period weaponry, it can change weapons but thinks they are harmful but they aren't). It isn't interesting to read, and it doesn't add to th SCP.

The dumb-but-show-accurate tantrums also really don't fit with his tone in the conversation on page 2. I wouldn't change the conversation, because that's definitely the best part.

Other than that… You're being too clever by half. The altering of the -A document immediately reminded me of the crown cognitihazard, which isn't a comparison you want to invite: you'll lose. You also never mentioned within the -A document that it alters it's own document format, so there's this vague meta level of confusion on top of the rest of the vagueness going on.
- So, you might as well make the picture match the actor, given that our expectations are 100% altered. That, or ditch the format changes, or push the format changes even further towards being Monty Python-esque, rather than just being slightly modified Foundation format. I guess the main way to do that would be, I donno, pictures, or "Sir 2476 the Safe" instated of the split lines.

No vote for now. I love the characterisation, but this needs cleanup.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a problem for me, because it was obvious that it was going there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cognihazards affects alter perceived information. Including the physical description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The one thing that really gets me, and I'll admit I mostly skimmed, is the assertion the guy is identical to Graham Chapman. Even with the censor bar, it's obvious he doesn't look a thing like Chapman. Is that intentional?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy crap I just realized why Mr Monty Python and the Holy Grail is actually Mr Spanish inquisition. It's because nobody expects the Spanish Inquisition.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmmm… I'm not so sure I'm onboard with this one. Feels like a bad -J rehash of concepts that were explored better and more thoroughly by other scips. No vote for now, since I may be missing something here, but overall not impressed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, my second point seems like mainly a wording change; I'm a little confused as to how this is supposed to look (I've never seen the sketch), so I can't offer anything on that.

For my third point, it'd require an entirely new interview, moving it in a different direction…I'd personally prefer someone more belligerent and, well, abrupt. I'd honestly like something punchier and more humorous than a bland re-re-re-explanation of GAW's overall shtick.

Lastly, the first document would ideally reflect the 'real' document better. Given that the real skip assaults people ineffectively for perceived slights, maybe have the first document's 'ability' relate to that in some way? You could try something with the Black Knight, I suppose?

The -1 effect isn't 'people perceive themselves and others to be reciting a script', it's 'information learned about it thematically fits MPATHG'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright, no worries man. Thanks for the feedback. Any suggestions how I could fix it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You've been hit by
You've been hit by
A smooth criminal

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well. While I would love to upvote given the overhaul on the first document alone, there's still a number of problems keeping me from doing so.

First, I still feel like the abilities of doc1 are too different from the abilities in -2. I think it would have been more logical for them to be -2's abilities perception-shifted into a MPATHG context. This goes double for the interview. I think going out of your way to tailor the -1 portion into a 'veiled by cognitohazard' version of the actuality of -2 would have made the reveal that much more satisfying.

Second, I think the second document's effects are oddly complicated: So, he sees potential weapons as safe items, but at the same time, sees them as being harmful, and still attempts to attack people with them? How exactly do knives take on the weight and texture of a cushion, still remain physically the same, yet not be harmful if you assault someone with it? I guess it's part of the joke, but the description of his effects seems weird.

Third, and this is my biggest gripe of all, the second interview. I'm just so damn disinterested in this skip's wistful memories of his creators and how lovingly thoughtful they are; and over-explaining the joke. I feel like it does nothing but reiterate for the umpteenth flipping time: 'gee, aren't these GAW guys just so sincere and funny? They sure do funny things because humor is something they get. You like humor and jokes, yes? You'll like them. Why, they are just like you and me.'. Christ, just no. The whole thing grinds to a screeching halt here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait a sec… Just Has The Tattoo is taken! The Hell! Right under our noses!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Literal Serial Killer, Get Anything for Free in Any Shop, and Just Has The Tattoo. I'll take care of them.
Didn't notice the link to 04. Added the others though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: sure, which ones?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
36. Sir Not Appearing In This Series

what are you planning Penton

You seem to be missing a few links in the list of misters. Mind if I add them in?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can't say I expected that ending. No, for reals….

Fuck it, upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If anyone's wondering, yes his dialogue in the first interview is comprised of words from the script.
